metadata.title:
CHARTER RECEIVES


GOOD BIG BOOST

Recall Provisions Remedied---Civil
Service Adopted---W Is


Completed.

pages.raw_text:
The charter committee practi-
calIy closed its Iabors last night,
passing upon the final draft of the
proposed city charter. The COM
mittee celebrated its meeting by
amending the direct legislation fea-
lures Of the proposed charter in
way to popularize the new docu-
ment with the people.


The percentage necessary to Sc
cure a special election to recall a
member of the proposed commits
sion was reduced from % per cent,
as now in force, to 20 per cent.
The proposition to compel voters to
go to the city clerks office in order
to sign a petition to recall an cleo
tive officer was also knocked out
and the requirement that signers
be registered voters was also done
away with. The petition under the
proposed charter can be given gen
eral circulation, and every objec-
tionable feature which has been
proposed was eliminated.


The initiative and referendum
features of the proposed charter
were also amended, the voters not
having to register in order to quail
ify as signers of petition. The
percentages to initiate or secure a
referendum vote remain as at pres
ent, 10 and s per cent respectively


The progressive members of the
committee got together last night
for the first time since the commit
tee has been meeting, with the re-
suit that the people can under the
proposed charter absolutely con-
troll their elective officers and can
legislate in all matters of interest
to the public, whenever such action
is deemed necessary.


The committee also went on rec
ord in favor of civil service for
members of the police and fire de.
partments The chiefs of the de-
partments are included in the pro-
posed civil service law. 1t is likely
that some opposition will develop
to the proposition to include the
chiefs of the departments in the
civil service. If this is done these
departments of public service will
be practically Independent of the
city commissioners, and the powers
of the commissioner of public
safety will be curtailed and the
efficiency of the department ham
pered by placing the chiefs under
civil service. 1t was the consensus
of opinion on the part of the com
mittee that the members of the
police and fire departments should
be promoted whenever occasion ar
rived to make changes or created
new positions in these departments


If the plan OF the committee is
carried out a member of the police
or fire departments can serve as
chief and retain his right to resume
his old position on the force should
he be retired as chief.
